news.summary: Energy Fuels has boosted its predicted uranium production for 2025 by 22% and its finished goods inventory guidance by a significant 193%. Additionally, the company has strengthened its working capital position. They are making progress in the production of high-grade U.S. uranium and world-class rare earth and mineral sand projects.
news.headline: Energy Fuels boosts 2025 uranium production by 22% and inventory by 193%, enhancing financial position and advancing key projects.

news.summary: Palo Alto, California - In a recent development, Institutional Property Advisors (IPA) has announced an expansion in Northern California. The move signifies IPA's increasing presence in the region. The specific details regarding this expansion, such as the exact locations involved, the number of properties included, and the financial figures associated with the expansion, have not been disclosed in the provided information. This expansion could potentially impact the real estate market in Northern California and bring new opportunities for investors and clients in the area.
news.headline: Institutional Property Advisors expands in Northern California, including Palo Alto, with a focus on growth.

news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia on May 7, 2025, Western Forest Products Inc. (TSX: WEF) disclosed the outcomes of its Annual General and Special Meeting of Shareholders. The meeting, which took place on the mentioned date, reported on the voting results.
news.headline: Western Forest Products Inc. (TSX: WEF) reports Annual General and Special Meeting voting results in Vancouver.

news.summary: MCAN Mortgage Corporation, also known as MCAN Financial Group, reported a net income of $16.6 million for the first quarter of 2025, down from $23.2 million in the same period in 2024. The decrease was mainly due to higher provisions for credit losses influenced by economic forecasts and geopolitical factors. Despite this, the company remains optimistic due to its strong liquidity, credit quality, and growth strategies. The return on average shareholders' equity for Q1 2025 was 10.99%, compared to 17.09% in Q1 2024. Total assets reached $5.4 billion, with non-securitized assets increasing to $3.0 billion, uninsured residential mortgages totaling $1.1 billion, and construction portfolio at $1.1 billion. Securitized mortgages decreased to $2.4 billion. In financial updates, net non-securitized mortgage spread income decreased by $1.7 million, while net securitized mortgage spread income increased by $0.6 million. There was a provision for credit losses on non-securitized mortgages of $3.1 million in Q1 2025 due to economic concerns impacting performing loans. The company aims to capitalize on growth opportunities amidst economic challenges, focusing on value creation for stakeholders.
news.headline: MCAN Financial Group's Q1 2025 net income was $16.6 million, down from $23.2 million in Q1 2024. They declared a 41 cents cash dividend. Total assets reached $5.4 billion, and uninsured residential mortgage originations increased to $97 million in Q1 2025. Non-securitized assets rose by 5.1% to $3.0 billion. Net non-securitized mortgage spread income dropped by $1.7 million. Net securitized mortgage spread income increased by $0.6 million. Provision for credit losses was $3.1 million in Q1 2025. CEO Derek Sutherland stated they are managing controllable factors to protect their bottom line and capitalize on opportunities in uncertain economic conditions.

news.summary: In Chicago on May 7, 2025, a record-setting rate hike request of $308.6 million by Nicor Gas is under scrutiny by advocacy groups. The Citizens Utility Board (CUB), Illinois PIRG, and Environmental Defense have submitted testimony to state regulators, stating that the proposed increase is excessive. They recommend reducing the rate hike by over 50%, highlighting concerns about the impact on consumers.
news.headline: Consumer advocates seek to reduce Nicor Gas' $308.6M rate hike by more than half, citing excessive profits and wasteful bonuses.

news.summary: The latest drilling results have revealed the highest grade yet with 60.8g/t AuEq over 0.4m, comprising of 51.3g/t Au, 7.2% Cu, and 18.0g/t Ag. The mineralization remains open, indicating a potential for further exploration. Additionally, there are assays pending from more drill holes, raising anticipation for potentially more positive results.
news.headline: Highly promising Golden Eye prospect with significant gold and copper, highest grade result of 60.8g/t AuEq, 51.3g/t Au, 7.2% Cu, 18.0g/t Ag; more results pending.

The company completed its second annual share buyback program, indicating a strong financial position. With low corporate break-evens, enduring assets, and a solid balance sheet, Athabasca is well-equipped to navigate through market fluctuations.

(TSX: TFPM, NYSE: TFPM) reported that all nine director nominees were successfully elected during the Annual Meeting of Shareholders on May 7, 2025. Dawn Whittaker received 183,446,362 votes in favor, representing 99.8% of votes cast. Voting details for other nominees can be found in the comprehensive results.

The limited-edition Miracle Can will go on sale starting May 8th at chosen stores. For further details, visit...

The company, a major exporter of fresh beef and its by-products in South America, achieved notable growth in its financial performance.
news.headline: Minerva Foods reports EBITDA of R$962.5 million in Q1 2025 with gross revenue of R$11.9 billion, up by 55% YoY.

news.summary: LNG Energy Group Corp. has announced a delay in filing its annual financial statements, management’s discussion and analysis, and related officer certifications for the financial year ended December 31, 2024, as well as Form 51-101F1, Form 51-101F2, and Form 51-101F3, as required by National Instrument 51-101. These filings were due by April 30, 2025, but the company attributes the delay to challenges in completing its local operational audit and recent departures of key personnel. LNG Energy Group is working with its Canadian auditors to finalize the Required Filings, which are expected to be submitted within two months of the original deadline. The company's stock is listed on multiple exchanges, including TSXV (LNGE), TSXV (LNGE.WT), OTCQB (LNGNF), and FWB (E26).
news.headline: LNG Energy Group delays filing required financial documents by 2 months due to audit and personnel issues.

news.summary: Taber, Alberta, May 7, 2025 - Flexible Solutions International, Inc. (NYSE-American: FSI) is a company specializing in biodegradable polymers for various industries such as oil extraction, detergent production, water treatment, and crop nutrient availability. They are also known for their environmentally friendly water and energy conservation technologies. Additionally, FSI is expanding into the food and nutrition supplement sectors. Today, the company revealed a special dividend of ten cents.
news.headline: FSI announces ten cent special dividend in Taber, Alberta on May 7, 2025 - manufacturer of biodegradable polymers and water conservation tech.

news.summary: A software company based in Fort Worth, Texas, called AcquireROI, has secured a growth debt package to support its expansion. AcquireROI specializes in offering performance-driven talent acquisition and recruitment marketing solutions. The company is a prominent provider in its field and is focused on growth. This move will aid in further developing the company and is an indicator of their commitment to progress.
news.headline: AcquireROI secures funding from Decathlon Capital Partners to fuel expansion with growth debt package in Fort Worth.

news.summary: In The Woodlands, Texas, on May 7, 2025, Kraton Corporation unveiled plans for a strategic shift in its operations to enhance its emphasis on key strengths. As a prominent worldwide manufacturer of specialty polymers and premium biobased items sourced from pine chemicals, the company aims to streamline its activities for more effective execution. This move underscores Kraton's commitment to optimizing its core competencies.
news.headline: Kraton Corp exits Dover site to focus on core business growth. Specializing in polymers and biobased products.

news.summary: A new study has found that the global population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, with the majority of the growth expected to occur in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. The study also predicts that by 2100, the population could reach 11.2 billion, highlighting the rapid pace of global population growth. This trend could have significant implications for food security, urbanization, and resource management in the coming decades. Experts suggest that timely interventions and policies will be crucial in addressing the challenges posed by this population growth.
news.headline: Rubicon Organics completes private placement offering under listed issuer financing exemption.

news.summary: In Hesperia, California on May 7, 2025, Pacific Gas and Electric Company participated in a groundbreaking event focusing on autonomous wildfire prevention. The gathering included top professionals in firefighting, state and federal officials, philanthropic organizations, insurance companies, and electric utilities. This marked the first of its kind event, highlighting the collaboration between different sectors to address the pressing issue of wildfires.
news.headline: PG&E joins autonomous wildfire suppression demo in CA with top officials & professionals.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Osisko Development Corp. (NYSE: ODV, TSXV: ODV) shared outcomes from its recent annual and special shareholder meeting. With 80,489,188 common shares voted, around 58.9% of the total outstanding shares as of the meeting's record date participated. Here are the key results from the meeting:
news.headline: Osisko Development reports results of shareholders' meeting with 80,489,188 shares voted (58.9% of total).

news.summary: Black Hills Corp. reaffirmed their 2025 earnings guidance of $4.00 to $4.20 per share, projecting 4-6% EPS growth rate. They achieved all rights-of-way for a 260-mile electric transmission expansion in Wyoming, aiming for long-term cost stability and customer growth. They served a new all-time peak load at Wyoming Electric, with nearly 10% increase over 2024, especially from data centers and blockchain growth. Seeking $35 million new annual revenues in Nebraska for reliable service. Wildfire legislation in Wyoming protects utilities from damages. In Q1 2025, they reported operating income of $205.0 million and net income of $134.3 million, at $1.87 per share, slightly higher than Q1 2024. Black Hills Corp. confident in regulatory and growth initiatives, with a strong liquidity position and solid performance in customer service. They made progress on transmission and generation projects, targeting 4-6% EPS growth. Black Hills stands by its 2025 earnings guidance and continues to focus on utility rate base growth and data center demand. Earnings are projected to reach over 10% by 2028. Dividend of $0.676/share approved for Q2 2025. Moody's Investor Service affirmed their long-term issuer rating.
news.headline: Black Hills Corp reports 2025 Q1 results, reaffirms guidance of $4.00 to $4.20 EPS, with Q1 operating income $205.0M.

news.summary: Lundin Mining Corporation reported a strong first quarter in 2025 with 76,774 tonnes of copper and 31,849 ounces of gold produced. Revenue was nearly $1 billion, with $388 million adjusted EBITDA and $337 million adjusted operating cash flow. The sale of European assets for $1.4 billion strengthened the balance sheet. They introduced a shareholder distribution policy targeting $220 million annually. The joint acquisition formed Vicuña Corp., with significant Mineral Resource estimates. The company reaffirmed guidance and expects operations to meet targets. Assets sold generated $180.1 million in revenue. Gross profit was $308.9 million. Adjusted earnings from continuing operations were $93.9 million. Cash provided by operations decreased to $122.3 million due to working capital changes. Sustaining capital expenditures decreased to $112.6 million. Free cash flow was $(47.5) million. Production details show positive performance at Candelaria, Caserones, and other sites. 2025 guidance includes production of 303,000 - 330,000 tonnes of copper and cash costs of $2.05 - $2.30 per pound. Lundin Mining is poised for continued growth and operational success.
news.headline: Lundin Mining's Q1 2025 results: Revenue $963.9M, copper production 76,774 tonnes, gold production 31,849 ounces. Initiatives like asset sales, new policies, key acquisitions made. Focused on cost control, targeting $220M annual shareholder returns, strong strategic position noted. Gazprom reaffirms 2025 guidance for production, cost, and capital. Operations on track with expectations. Read full article on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, in St. Augustine, Florida, ATrack Solutions, a prominent provider of ABA-specific clinical and operational software, revealed that it has been chosen as a Business Affiliate of the Council of Autism Service Providers (CASP). This selection marks a significant achievement for ATrack Solutions in gaining recognition within the autism service provider community. CASP, a well-known national organization, offers valuable resources and support to its members working in the field of autism services. This collaboration positions ATrack Solutions to enhance its reach and impact within the autism service providers' network, benefiting from CASP's established platform and network.
news.headline: ATrack Solutions chosen as Business Affiliate by CASP, a prominent autism service provider council.

news.summary: In Greenwood Village, Colorado on May 7, 2025, Century Communities, Inc. (NYSE: CCS), a leading homebuilder in the United States, revealed that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.29 per share. Shareholders are set to receive this dividend on June 11, 2025.

news.summary: Atlanta-based Newell Brands Inc. declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.07 per share. This dividend will be payable on June 13, 2025, to common stockholders recorded on May 30, 2025. Newell Brands (NASDAQ: NWL) is a prominent consumer goods company with popular brands like Rubbermaid, Sharpie, Graco, Coleman, Rubbermaid Commercial Products, Yankee Candle, Paper Mate, and Foo.
news.headline: Newell Brands (NWL) declares $0.07 quarterly dividend per share, payable on June 13, 2025 to stockholders of record on May 30, 2025.

news.summary: Reviving production, robust cash flow, and monetizing hedges have led to a reduction in debt. These initiatives have proven highly successful, resulting in a significant decrease in our debt levels. The strategic move to reactivate production has yielded positive results, along with harnessing the strong cash flow generated by our operations. Furthermore, smartly monetizing hedges has also played a key role in driving down our debt. These combined efforts have effectively lowered our debt to a notable extent, providing a more stable financial outlook for the company.
news.headline: Pieridae's Q1 2025 results show lower debt due to reactivated production, strong cash flow, and hedge monetization.

news.summary: An optimistic forecast has been made by crypto entrepreneur Arthur Hayes, predicting a potential surge in Bitcoin to record levels this year. Hayes foresees all necessary conditions being met for this anticipated rally in the cryptocurrency market.
news.headline: Bitcoin poised for a rally as per Arthur Hayes due to Fed influence, with potential for record highs.

news.summary: Dine Brands Global Inc. (NYSE: DIN) saw a rise in shares following the release of their first-quarter earnings report. The company experienced a 4.1% year-on-year revenue growth in the first quarter of FY25, reaching $214.78 million, slightly below the expected $217.56 million as per analyst estimates. This growth was largely propelled by an increase in company restaurant sales, primarily from acquiring 47 Applebee's restaurants in Q4 of 2024. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $1.03, falling short of the projected $1.23. The detailed article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Dine Brands Global Inc. stock gained after reporting Q1 FY25 revenue growth of 4.1% to $214.78 million, missing estimate. EPS of $1.03 missed at $1.23.

news.summary: Sydney's Vivid Sydney 2025, the biggest arts and culture festival in the Southern Hemisphere, will feature the debut joint performance of popular TV personalities Nick Offerman and Megan Mullally on stage. The renowned husband-and-wife comedy duo will showcase their talents in an unscripted show.
news.headline: Television icons Nick Offerman and Megan Mullally to perform together at Vivid Sydney 2025, Southern Hemisphere's largest festival.

news.summary: In San Jose, California on May 7, 2025, Harmonic disclosed that Nimrod Ben-Natan, President and CEO, and Walter Jankovic, CFO, will conduct virtual investor sessions at the 20th Annual Needham Virtual Technology, Media & Consumer event. Harmonic is listed on NASDAQ as HLIT.
news.headline: Harmonic's CEO and CFO will participate in virtual investor meetings at two conferences.

news.summary: Claros Mortgage Trust, Inc. (NYSE: CMTG) shared its financial results for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025, in New York. The Company disclosed a GAAP net loss of $78.6 million, equivalent to $0.56 per share, for the same period. In terms of Distributable Loss, a non-GAAP financial metric, the reported figure was $35.7 million, or $0.25 per share. Additionally, Distributable Earnings before realized losses were $11.6 million, translating to $0.08 per share for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025.
news.headline: Claros Mortgage Trust, Inc. (NYSE: CMTG) reported Q1 2025 financial results: GAAP net loss $78.6M, Distributable Loss $35.7M.

news.summary: The latest figures show that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the country, with over 10,000 new cases reported in the past 24 hours. This brings the total number of cases to nearly 200,000. Additionally, there have been 150 new deaths, bringing the total number of fatalities to over 5,000. Health officials are urging the public to continue following safety guidelines to prevent further spread of the virus.
news.headline: Sila Realty Trust reveals Q1 2025 results, marking a profitable start to the year.

news.summary: Dream Residential Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: DRR.U, TSX: DRR.UN) shared its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025. The Comparative Properties Net Operating Income (NOI) for Q1 2025 stood at $6.1 million, marking a 0.8% growth compared to the previous quarter. The company is set to hold a conference call on May 8, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. (ET) to further discuss these results.
news.headline: Dream Residential REIT's Q1 2025 results show comparative properties NOI of $6.1 million, up 0.8% from previous quarter.
